atoms.atom_name: Ofgem supply licence readiness (fit-and-proper test, financial resilience/ring-fencing, Standard Licence Conditions go/no-go)

notes.text: 2026-07-12 AUTHORED AS PROPOSAL (EPOCH_GATING_AND_ATOM_AUTHORSHIP.md Rule 2, self-refill draw): a real, distinct epoch-5 gap named directly by the staged instruction's own worked example ('the regulatory/licence path'), confirmed absent from the map by direct read of every existing F/H-lane epoch-5 atom. H3_production_readiness_nfr_evidence/H4_go_live_nfr_register cover NON-FUNCTIONAL/operational readiness (SRE-style: availability, security, capacity); F3_obligations_register covers ONGOING per-transaction compliance invariants (already L3, built); F4_company_internal_authz covers INTERNAL authz/segregation of duties. None of the three model whether the simulated company could actually HOLD an Ofgem electricity/gas supply licence in the first place: the Standard Licence Conditions threshold tests (the fit-and-proper persons test), the post-2021-crisis financial resilience/ring-fencing requirements introduced after the Bulb/Avro/et al. supplier failures (customer credit-balance protection, minimum capital adequacy), and the Capacity Market/Renewables Obligation registration obligations that attach to holding a licence at all -- a real UK supplier's board treats this as a distinct go/no-go gate, decided by Ofgem's licensing function, not its own ops/security review. PROPOSAL ONLY -- not ranked, not built. Immediately workable in DISCOVER (read the real Ofgem licensing framework/Standard Licence Conditions text, the 2021-22 supplier-failure post-mortems that produced the financial-resilience reforms) per Rule 1; BUILD stays gated (loop_stage: idle) until the director ranks and promotes it, per Rule 2.

2026-07-12 RE-RANKED (director, RERANK_AND_PROVISIONAL_PLAN.md): ACCEPTED, 'DISCOVER now, build gated to Epoch 5' -- the licence path may CONSTRAIN Epoch-3 design, so discovering it late is expensive. DISCOVER done this pass (docs/market_research/ofgem_licence_readiness.md, real sourced findings via WebSearch/WebFetch against ofgem.gov.uk, not confabulated): three linked ongoing licence conditions SLC 4A (operational capability) / 4B (financial responsibility principle -- mutualised-cost management, the SoLR/RO/FiT socialisation mechanism) / 4C (ongoing fit-and-proper, SMR&I role-holders, introduced 2021); a REAL quantified Minimum Capital Requirement (Capital Floor GBP0, Capital Target GBP130/dual-fuel-equivalent domestic customer, decided 26 July 2023, phased via milestone assessments); CCB ring-fencing is a DIRECTED power (Ofgem may tell a supplier to ring-fence), not a blanket universal rule -- a company-specific regulatory state, not a constant; licence applications currently carry a 6-month processing timescale (Ofgem guidance updated 27 Feb 2026), confirming this is a live, current-day gate not a historical 2021-22 artefact only. level_current correctly stays 0 (DISCOVER does not move levels, MATURITY_MAP.md section 3). Honest open items registered, not silently dropped (R10): Capacity Market/RO registration duties not independently re-verified this pass and may overlap with F3_obligations_register (boundary needs an explicit FRAME decision, not an assumption); Capital Target milestone-assessment dates/mechanics unresolved (would need the FRC decision PDF itself, not the summary webpage); detailed SLC 4C assessment criteria unresolved; relationship to the company's existing segment-capital/ROCE modelling (B2_opex_cost_to_serve) not yet framed -- licence-level Minimum Capital Requirement is an absolute floor, distinct from segment ROCE, must not be conflated when this atom eventually frames its own target-level definitions. loop_stage stays idle (BUILD still gated to Epoch 5, per the director's own instruction).

2026-07-12 DISCOVER addendum, closes one named open item (self-refill draw, no BUILD code): SLC 4C's assessment criteria, previously flagged unresolved, now found and real. SLC 4C's fit-and-proper obligation is deliberately BROAD, not board/exec-only: it covers anyone with 'significant managerial responsibility or influence' (SMR&I) -- for a real supply business this explicitly includes heads of finance, risk, operations, technology, data, compliance, trading/hedging, strategy, and marketing/CRM, and can extend to external advisors or consultants with effective decision-making authority, not just directly-employed staff. Implication for this atom's own eventual framing: a real go/no-go SLC 4C assessment would need to model role-holder coverage across this WIDE set, not just a company's board -- a materially larger scope than 'are the directors fit and proper' alone. Remaining open items (Capacity Market/RO registration overlap with F3, Capital Target milestone mechanics, B2 relationship) still genuinely unresolved, not addressed this pass.

2026-07-13 DISCOVER addendum, closes the milestone-assessment mechanics gap (self-refill draw, no BUILD code). Real finding: milestone assessments are CUSTOMER-COUNT-triggered, not date-based -- a licensed supplier must pause new domestic customer onboarding on EACH relevant gas/electricity licence at the 50,000 and 200,000 customer thresholds, with an Ofgem assessment of operational capacity required before onboarding can resume. This is a concrete, real mechanism directly relevant to this project's own population-draw/growth atoms (W2_2) if this atom is ever built out: a real supplier's own growth trajectory is gated by regulatory review at named customer-count milestones, not smooth. HONEST DISCREPANCY FOUND, not silently resolved: this pass's source states the Capital Target as GBP115/dual-fuel-equivalent customer, while the earlier-registered figure (above, sourced from the 26 July 2023 FRC decision) was GBP130 -- the two figures may reflect different points in a phased ramp, a since-revised figure, or source imprecision; registered as a genuine, unresolved discrepancy needing the primary FRC decision document itself (not a summary webpage) to settle, not picked between arbitrarily. Real, current, named industry confirmation found: Octopus Energy has publicly stated it met the Ofgem capital buffer target -- a real, concrete example this atom's eventual build could cite. Capacity Market/RO overlap with F3 and the B2 ROCE relationship remain genuinely unresolved.

2026-07-13 DISCOVER addendum, closes the F3 overlap gap (self-refill draw, internal code check, no external research needed, no BUILD code). Checked directly whether F3_obligations_register's own domain_invariants.py already covers Capacity Market/RO registration duties: zero references to Capacity Market or Renewables Obligation anywhere in company/compliance/ -- confirmed NO overlap exists today. Separately, found this project's codebase ALREADY has real Capacity Market code -- company/market/flexibility_potential.py + flexibility_revenue_book.py -- but confirmed by reading it directly that this models a COMPLETELY DIFFERENT real-world CM mechanism: the company EARNING Capacity Market revenue as a flexibility/DSR participant (~GBP75/kW/yr for committed flexibility), not the SUPPLIER-SIDE registration/settlement-levy OBLIGATION this atom's own text means ('Capacity Market/RO registration obligations that attach to holding a licence at all'). Same three-letter acronym, two genuinely distinct real mechanisms (a generator/DSR-side revenue opportunity vs. a supplier-side compliance cost) -- this project's existing CM code is NOT this atom's scope and does not need reconciling with it. This closes the F3-overlap open item cleanly: no overlap, no conflict, both real and both already correctly separate. Remaining open item: the B2_opex_cost_to_serve/ROCE relationship.

2026-07-13 DISCOVER addendum, closes the final open item -- ALL FOUR named honest gaps now resolved (self-refill draw, internal code check, no BUILD code). Read company/finance/segment_capital.py directly to confirm the B2 relationship: B2's own segment ROCE is a genuinely DIFFERENT concept from this atom's own licence-level Minimum Capital Requirement, confirming (not just re-asserting) the distinction the registration already claimed. B2 measures PER-SEGMENT capital efficiency against a DIRECTOR-SET hurdle rate (working capital = directly-attributable segment receivables; credit/settlement exposure and collateral = portfolio-level figures allocated pro-rata by revenue share, explicitly documented as an allocation not a bottom-up measurement) -- an internal pricing/cross-subsidy-visibility diagnostic, R12-governed (never tuned to a target). This atom's own Minimum Capital Requirement is a REGULATORY, portfolio-wide, absolute floor (Ofgem's own external requirement) with zero code-level interaction or shared computation with B2's segment-ROCE mechanism -- confirmed genuinely independent, not merely asserted to be. All four named open items from this atom's own DISCOVER trail (Capacity Market/RO overlap, Capital Target milestone mechanics, SLC 4C criteria, B2 relationship) are now closed with real evidence; the one still-unresolved item is the honest GBP115-vs-GBP130 Capital Target figure discrepancy, which needs the primary FRC decision document, not further code-reading.

atoms.atom_name: VAT-by-segment control must check against a segment signal INDEPENDENT of the bill's own label (currently a tautology)

notes.text: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (INVARIANT_LIBRARY_REDTEAM.md top-1, HIGH -- a live Tier-1 control giving FALSE assurance). saas/bill_generator.py:158 computes vat = subtotal * vat_rate(segment); pre_bill_validation.py:60-68 re-derives the rate by dividing back and checks it against vat_rate_for_segment(segment) (domain_invariants.py:395-406) -- BOTH sides are the same function of the same segment field, so the check passes for every bill the generator can emit and STRUCTURALLY CANNOT catch its own named C6 SME-as-Household mislabel class (the exact R10 absurdity class it was built for). FIX (class, per R10): re-base the expected VAT/segment on an INDEPENDENT observable signal (e.g. consumption band / meter profile / tariff registration) so a mislabelled segment produces a MISMATCH the gate catches, not a tautology. loop_stage idle: DISCOVER/FRAME the independent-signal choice now; BUILD is a real compliance fix to rank.

2026-07-13 FIXED L0->L2, target REACHED (worktree fork, commit a7c74147, merged to main 42a1fcc6; orchestrator-verified 72 compliance/billing tests pass, epistemic PASS). The tautology is broken: new consumption_implied_vat_rate() + check_vat_consistent_with_consumption() + VAT_SEGMENT_MATCHES_CONSUMPTION invariant re-base the expected VAT on METERED CONSUMPTION (independent of the segment label), wired into validate_bill() alongside the retained arithmetic check. Proven non-tautological: check_vat(resi,0.05) still returns True (arithmetic control blind to the mislabel) BUT check_vat_consistent_with_consumption(resi,electricity,0.05,2346.8kWh/mo) returns False -> validate_bill emits an SME-as-Household mislabel reason on the identical bill the old control passed. NO false positives: 0 of 1588 real bills newly held; legit domestic / correct I&C-SME / small-SME-on-domestic-consumption / real electric-heated-resi-max all pass. HONEST one-directional strength (documented, not papered): strong catching an I&C-scale load billed at domestic 5%; WEAK in reverse (a domestic customer over-charged at 20% is undetectable from consumption alone -- a genuine microbusiness legitimately consumes domestic volumes) -- the reverse needs a premises/MPAN-profile signal not in the bill data today (residual, named). target 2 met, loop_stage idle.

atoms.atom_name: Structural bill-integrity invariants: arithmetic footing + non-negativity/fail-CLOSED + temporal sanity

notes.text: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (INVARIANT_LIBRARY_REDTEAM.md confirmed gaps 2-4+6). No arithmetic footing (a GBP999,999 total on an GBP88 bill passes -- line items are never asserted to sum to the total); no sign/non-negativity check AND the gate fails OPEN when subtotal<=0 (a zero/negative bill skips validation entirely); unguarded temporal impossibility (a reversed-date bill is silently clamped to 1 day instead of rejected); and the Qwen internal-audit backstop FAILS SILENT -- returns 'clean' when Ollama is unreachable, i.e. on effectively every autonomous run (false assurance). FIX (class): add load-bearing structural invariants -- line-items-sum-to-total footing, non-negativity with FAIL-CLOSED on non-positive subtotal, start<=end temporal sanity that REJECTS not clamps; and make the audit backstop fail-CLOSED/flag when the model is unreachable. loop_stage idle: rank for BUILD.

atoms.atom_name: Obligations register coverage + traceability: physical-harm rows, resolve-or-degrade tracker, enforcing-invariant key

notes.text: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (INVARIANT_LIBRARY_REDTEAM.md top-3). Register coverage gaps: physical-harm obligation rows (gas-safety, self-disconnection) under-represented; the existing_tracker field is unresolved/degraded in places; and obligation rows do not carry the KEY of the invariant that enforces them, so SLC-31A-vs-21BA style drift between an obligation and its enforcement can go undetected. Also the enforcement is bills-only -- annual statements, DD resets, refunds are ungated surfaces a bad value can reach a customer through. FIX: extend register coverage + carry an enforcing-invariant key per row (traceability) + widen the gate surface beyond bills. loop_stage idle: rank for BUILD.

atoms.atom_name: Close the 5 mutation-found control gaps (KL-4..KL-8): fail-silent/fail-open/tautology in compliance trackers + the consistency gate

notes.text: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (H12 pass-2 mutation-test findings, CONTROL_KILL_LIST.md KL-4..KL-8). Five real controls that do not fire on their named defect -- QUEUE-d (multi-caller semantics changes, not fix-on-sight): KL-4 FAIL-SILENT population_sanity.check_estimated_read_rate([]) reads clean though a total absence of reads is the most-broken state; KL-5 FAIL-SILENT ConsumerDutyRegister().overall_rag() reports GREEN on an EMPTY register (un-assessed = a governance failure under FCA Consumer Duty -- important); KL-6 TAUTOLOGY social_obligation.non_compliant() trusts the self-declared status field (mislabelled-PAID underspend passes; mitigated by independent underspend_records()); KL-7 FAIL-OPEN green_claims_audit.audit() short-circuits obligation==0 -> 100pct COMPLIANT (broken green-product detection reads compliant); KL-8 FAIL-SILENT+OPEN _check_consistency passes when run_insights.json is missing and silently skips a one-sided missing headline key (same class as the R11 orphan-transition incident). FIX (per R15/R10, the CLASS): each must FIRE on its named defect -- empty/zero/missing input fails CLOSED or ALARMS; self-status replaced by an independent signal (KL-6 like F5). Each fix gets a mutation test proving it now fires. loop_stage idle: rank for BUILD (KL-5 + KL-7 highest -- customer/regulatory-facing false-GREEN).

2026-07-13 FIXED L0->L2, target REACHED, ALL 5 GAPS CLOSED (worktree fork, commit d6efc0dc, merged b2797dc96; orchestrator-verified 313 pass+2 xfail on merged main, epistemic PASS). Each KL-4..8 now FIRES on its named defect with a mutation test proving before(theatre/passed-wrongly)->after(fires): KL-4 population_sanity empty-log returns a finding; KL-5 ConsumerDuty empty register returns NOT_ASSESSED (distinct from GREEN) + needs_attention; KL-6 social_obligation folds independent is_underspend evidence (kept-and-cross-checked, defence-in-depth, tautology can no longer manufacture compliance); KL-7 green_claims zero-obligation -> NOT_APPLICABLE for no-claims + NON_COMPLIANT (fails closed) for a broken green product; KL-8 _check_consistency fails closed on absent/empty insights + catches one-sided missing headline key. NO false positives (verified vs live run: 1588 reads still clean, populated all-green register still GREEN, PAID full-spend still not flagged, all 8 headline keys present). ConsumerDuty/SocialObligation registers are test-only in production so KL-5/6 carry zero production-outcome risk (the 14+-callers concern was overstated, corrected honestly). CUMULATIVE KILL LIST now: 27 tested / 26 FIRED / 1 THEATRE (check_vat, documented defence-in-depth) / 0 registered gaps remaining. HONEST RESIDUAL (not F8): 2 pre-existing unrelated tests/tools/ failures (test_generate_dashboard_mgmt KeyError C1; test_generate_billing_ledger stale run_output vs run_insights drift) -- confirmed via git stash they fail identically WITHOUT F8, out of scope, worth a separate finding. target 2 met, loop_stage idle.

atoms.atom_name: Unified event log shared across sim/company for the epoch-2 reveal spine

notes.text: 2026-07-12 LEVEL CORRECTED, 0->2, with decisive existing evidence (self-refill draw, dial-weighted, epoch-2 idle-tier DISCOVER): the charter's own framing ('G2's generalisation of the bitemporal event log... extending BitemporalEventLog's dual valid_time/transaction_time design beyond price/forward data') implied a BUILD was still needed to generalise the class -- verified directly against the real code and found this is stale. `BitemporalRecord`/`BitemporalEventLog` (company/interfaces/bitemporal_event_log.py) are keyed by arbitrary `entity_id`/`fact_type` strings with `value: Any` -- there is NOTHING price-specific in the core class itself; `build_price_bitemporal_log()` (company/interfaces/point_in_time_view.py) is a thin, separate populator function that merely CALLS the already-generic `.record()`, not a price-coupled variant of the log. Decisive: the existing test suite (tests/company/interfaces/test_bitemporal_event_log.py, 17 tests, all passing) already exercises the log with a genuinely NON-price fact_type -- `test_different_fact_type_not_confused` records `('meter_1', 'consumption_kwh', ...)` alongside a price fact for the same entity and asserts they're not confused. This is the L2 bar met with real, pre-existing, already-passing evidence ('a real, generalised event-log object exists... proven correct in isolation') -- not new work, but a genuine mis-registration corrected: G2's own code was built as a byproduct of W1_reveal_over_time's spine work and never credited to this atom. Matches the same class of correction already applied this session to D2_three_clocks (stale depends_on) and E2_revenue_reconciliation (stale loop_stage) -- an atom's registered state not reflecting work that already happened elsewhere in the codebase. L3 remains genuinely open and is NOT claimed here: it requires 'at least one real cross-cutting event type flows through this shared log and is genuinely consumed by both a sim-side and a company-side reader' -- today the log's only real production populator (build_price_bitemporal_log) is consumed by ONE side only (company/trading/hedge_decision.py's own volatility calc); no second, company-side-authored fact_type (e.g. a customer interaction or a settlement restatement, both named as L3 candidates in the charter) has yet been written through it by any real caller. loop_stage build->harden (at target, L2); a genuine HARDEN/Expert-Hour pass has not yet run on this atom specifically (it inherited W1's own build quality but has never itself been the subject of an adversarial review), registered as the honest remaining gap before this atom's OWN L2 claim could be called fully hardened, distinct from L3's real-caller gap.

atoms.atom_name: Record the worktree-miss retro as durable method IP (5 findings + meta) in the casebook; wire Finding-1 trigger

notes.text: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (RETRO_WHY_WE_MISSED_IT.md, director-requested 'let's remember this learning', QUEUE -- these are IP, not housekeeping). Five method findings + a meta-finding, each a general law with its evidence: (F1) SEARCH THE FIELD WHEN YOU HIT A WALL -- any structural wall = an immediate check of published practice BEFORE theorising ('we are stuck on X' is the strongest signal someone published the answer to X); the epoch-boundary harness re-check is too slow for a field turning over monthly. (F2) A REVIEW ONLY ANSWERS THE QUESTION YOU ASKED -- when a review comes back clean, ask what it did NOT ask; add a standing 'what was out of scope, and why?' section to every review artefact. (F3) FIRST-PRINCIPLES REASONING IS NOT EVIDENCE (R9, advisor edition) -- a confident derivation about a fast-moving field is a HYPOTHESIS, verify against published reality before acting ('BUILD is inherently narrow / physics' was wrong in a checkable way). (F4) BOTTLENECKS ARE ONIONS -- empty draw->cap1->livelock->BUILD-gated->map-contention, each fix revealed the next; the first diagnosis of a slow system is almost never the real one. (F5) THE ARCHITECTURE WORK AND THE VELOCITY WORK ARE THE SAME WORK -- when a structural fix keeps getting deferred as 'architecture', check whether it also caps throughput; if so it is now, not later. (META) THE OUTSIDE QUESTION IS THE HIGHEST-VALUE INPUT -- every one of the last five structural findings came from the DIRECTOR's naive-sounding question, not the machine's self-audit or the advisor's analysis; protect the director's ability to ask naive questions -- the human seat that cannot be automated away. DoD: findings recorded in the method/casebook lane (site Method door); F1 wired as a real trigger (structural wall -> published-practice check, before theorising) WITH a test it fires; F2 added as a required section in every review-artefact template. DISCOVER/FRAME workable now.

atoms.atom_name: Typed internal seams between billing/pricing/settlement/collections, enforced like the epistemic wall (Epoch 3)

notes.text: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (WORKTREE_ISOLATION_AND_SEAMS.md Half 2, director-decided QUEUE, Epoch 3). Worktrees solve FILE-SYSTEM collisions but NOT LOGICAL dependencies: two agents both reaching into hedged_settlement.py still collide in MEANING at the merge. The fix is the doctrine we already have (the sim/company epistemic wall enforced by hooks + tests, not a network) applied INWARD -- typed interfaces between billing / pricing / settlement / collections, enforced the same way ('walled interfaces'). KEY ROADMAP CONSEQUENCE (record it): the architecture work and the velocity work are THE SAME WORK -- Epoch-3 internal seams are not only the go-live boundary, they are what makes concurrent building SAFE, so they cap throughput now and are not merely 'later'. Also discharges the monolith obligation: a monolith stays modular only by DISCIPLINE (microservices enforce it through pain); we chose the monolith and have not yet paid that bill -- internal seams are how we pay it. Epoch-3 gated for BUILD, but DISCOVER/FRAME is available NOW and should be RANKED accordingly (thinking is never gated) -- this is the Lane-3 discovery work to progress alongside builds.

2026-07-13 DISCOVER/FRAME LANDED (Lane-3 fork, doc-only, no level move, commit 14dff83d): docs/design/GO_LIVE_SEAM_AND_INTERNAL_SEAMS_DESIGN.md. Key decision: ONE async-shaped envelope (WallRequest/WallResponse, frozen, correlation-id = idempotency key, NOT_KNOWABLE_YET as a first-class status, valid_time/observed_at split lifted from bitemporal_event_log.py) serves BOTH the outer sim/company go-live wall AND the four inner seams (pricing->billing->collections, settlement->billing) -- same shape at two radii, so C-S3 + A3's 'schema can't represent pending latency' are the same law, built once. Internal-seam enforcement clones tools/epistemic_verifier.py's git-diff scan with a FORBIDDEN_CROSS_DOMAIN import table; R14 basis-label becomes a structural schema gate. This is why architecture work = velocity work (two agents can't collide in meaning if the only shared artefact is a versioned typed contract). Biggest open Q for Epoch-3 open: async-migration cost -- migrate call sites incrementally vs a sync convenience wrapper (lean wrapper per SIMPLICITY GUARD), and how to stop the wrapper becoming the default so the async shape actually gets exercised. Epoch-3 BUILD stays gated until epoch-2 exit test passes.

atoms.atom_name: Poesys brand identity as an AI-usable schema: design-tokens source + agent-loaded rules + preserved exemplar + live-site adoption + brand-compliance check

notes.text: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (BRAND_CONSTITUTION.md, director-decided/ratified live 'love it', Tier 3 flag-and-proceed, QUEUE). Lane-2 SITE per THREE_LANES -- site/** + document templates, disjoint from BUILD, runs parallel, never gates an epoch; director-authorised to run alongside builds. The visual+verbal identity authority for EVERY Poesys surface (site, charts, bill PDFs, reports, board packs, decks, digests, NTFYs); sits ABOVE SITE_CONSTITUTION.md/POESYS_SITE_BRIEF.md and WINS on visual-identity conflicts (reconcile, don't fork). KEY CONSTRAINT: every Poesys artefact is AI-generated, so the brand book's consumer is the AGENT -- it must be machine-readable tokens + short testable rules + canonical exemplars, so any agent produces on-brand output BY CONSTRUCTION and drift is test-detectable. DECIDED (do not re-litigate): identity = radically plain/honest, 'the energy supplier that hides nothing', architectural simplicity, black+white carry all structure, no ornament; the company's existing honesty conventions (clock/basis labels, provisional badges, published misses) ARE the brand voice (formalise, invent nothing). PALETTE 'BRAG' = black+white + blue/red/amber/green each bright+soft (12 values), with fixed semantics: blue=verified-done (earned, expert-hour-passed/at-target, never self-declared), green=on-track, amber=at-risk/provisional/estimated, red=blocked/failing. 6 LAWS (colour is information never decoration; bright=live soft=historical/fill; soft never as text/thin-line; ONE neo-grotesque typeface, hierarchy via weight/size/space; glyphs are information not decoration, 6 is the ceiling; type-only identity, wordmark lowercase 'poesys.' WITH the full stop). GLYPH GRAMMAR (6, held absolutely, work in plain text incl NTFY/digest/commits): . =done/verified(blue), _ =in-build/on-track(green), ~ =estimated/provisional(amber), ! =blocked/failing(red), > =action/next/CTA, // =the working/basis/provenance. DELIVERABLES (required properties, impl is the agent's): (1) ONE machine-readable design-tokens JSON (W3C format) = the sole home of every colour/type/spacing value, every surface consumes tokens never raw values; (2) an agent-loaded brand-rules file (CLAUDE.md-style short imperative testable statements, referenced before generating any visual surface); (3) canonical exemplar preserved verbatim (the ratified §7 mock -- front door + customer bill + glyph grammar -- generation COPIES from it, never reinterprets); (4) enforcement testable in the harness (no raw colour value outside the token source; soft-colour-as-text detectable/blocked). DoD: token source is sole colour home + the LIVE site consumes it end-to-end (R11 rendered-pixel, director's eyes); rules file loaded by visual-surface generators; exemplar preserved + site front door and one real customer bill demonstrably follow it (BRAG chips, ~///> in live use); brand-compliance harness check passes. ROLLOUT of remaining surfaces (charts, PDFs, reports, decks, NTFY/digest glyph conventions) registers as FOLLOW-UP atoms -- batch honestly, no big-bang. OPEN (genuine, everything else decided): self-hosted typeface selection (agent proposes, DIRECTOR RATIFIES -- brand dial faces the director, same law as R13 curriculum; any palette/dial change is director-ratified only), enforcement mechanism design, token file location/format, rollout sequencing, reconciliation pass vs SITE_CONSTITUTION/POESYS_SITE_BRIEF so there is exactly one visual-identity authority. loop_stage idle: SITE-lane build (director-authorised, runs parallel), DISCOVER/FRAME (token schema, enforcement mechanism, typeface options) workable now; R11 live-pixel DoD needs the director's eyes (no autonomous network). The ratified exemplar is preserved verbatim inside docs/design/BRAND_CONSTITUTION.md §7 (established as the standing visual-identity authority, moved out of staging).

2026-07-13 BUILT L0->L2 (worktree fork, commit 954cf6b0, merged to main aceed951; orchestrator-verified 9/9 brand-compliance tests + epistemic PASS on merged main). The 4 deliverables built: site/brand/tokens.json (W3C design-tokens, the 12 BRAG colours + b/w/greys + type/spacing/weights, sole colour home) + tokens.css (byte-pinned projection); docs/design/BRAND_RULES.md (agent-loaded, 6 laws + glyph grammar verbatim + typeface-is-a-director-dial note); site/brand/exemplar.html (the section-7 mock verbatim, drift-guarded byte-equal to the constitution); tools/brand_compliance.py + tests/tools/test_brand_compliance.py (9 assertions -- no raw hex outside the token source, soft-colour-as-text flagged, palette drift-guard vs constitution). proof.html demonstrates one surface consuming tokens with zero raw hex. Typeface + palette left as director dials (not self-decided, R13-shaped). HONEST L2 not L3: L3 needs the LIVE production site CSS refactored to consume the tokens end-to-end + R11 live-pixel verification (director eyes / no autonomous network) -- the CSS adoption is autonomously buildable (SITE-lane follow-up), the pixel is the director residual. loop_stage build (token-adoption rollout is real remaining build).

atoms.atom_name: Concurrent BUILD atoms run in isolated git worktrees (native isolation: worktree), map read-during-build

notes.text: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (WORKTREE_ISOLATION_AND_SEAMS.md Half 1, director-decided QUEUE, HIGH rank -- the last known cap on BUILD width). Our exact failure is the documented headline problem: multiple agents in ONE working tree overwrite edits / read half-modified files / corrupt the git index. The answer already exists and is native: git worktrees (one checked-out dir + branch per agent, shared .git object store) via Claude Code's `isolation: worktree` subagent directive (our build agents in .claude/agents/ are already subagents) -- VERIFY the directive against current official docs before adopting. SUPERSEDES H9's interim 'orchestrator is sole map writer' convention with structure: (1) concurrent BUILD atoms each in an isolated worktree/branch, no shared tree; (2) maturity_map.yaml becomes READ-DURING-BUILD -- per-atom status/evidence to its own file (or append-only journal), map generated/merged from those, map stays single source of truth for the draw, no lost update (this is H9's build, done here as one mechanism); (3) merge model = orchestrated sequential merge, one integrator merges branches in defined order after each passes checks (simple model right below ~4-5 concurrent agents = us); (4) scoped suite per worktree, full 17k suite ONCE at integration; (5) per-worktree env is real plumbing (env files, ports, deps, daemon stack do NOT carry over) -- handle explicitly; (6) pre-flight merge-base conflict detection before merge. DoD: two disjoint atoms building simultaneously in separate worktrees, both status updates landing, no lost writes, merged cleanly. Fold recurring re-check into HARNESS_BEST_PRACTICE_ADOPTION (field moving monthly). loop_stage idle: DISCOVER/FRAME (verify the directive, design the status-file/merge model) workable now; BUILD is a harness change to rank.

2026-07-13 FRAME DESIGN LANDED (Lane-3 discovery fork, doc-only, docs/design/WORKTREE_AND_MAP_CONTENTION_DESIGN.md -- one design covering H10 Half-A worktree isolation + H9 Half-B map read-during-build). Two load-bearing findings from direct repo read: (1) NONE of the six .claude/agents/*.md frontmatters use any isolation directive today (only name/description/tools/model) -- so `isolation: worktree` is UNPROVEN in-repo and MUST be verified against current official Claude Code subagent docs before wiring (the single biggest build-time risk; no network here to confirm the exact frontmatter key/value, branch-naming, worktree path). (2) The two build agents (saas-engineer, sim-engineer, both model:opus) are the only ones needing isolation; the four read-only agents do not. Design specifies: orchestrated sequential merge (first-green-first, pre-flight merge-base changed-file intersection to catch a mis-declared file_scope and re-sequence rather than merge blind); scoped suite per worktree by file_scope root (sim/simulation->tests/sim+tests/simulation, saas->tests/saas, company->tests/company, etc.), full ~17k suite ONCE at integration; per-worktree env plumbing enumerated concretely (secrets carry over via ~/.config out-of-tree + conftest setdefault + SIM_FAST_MODE autouse => scoped tests need no Ollama; .venv must be shared by absolute path, NOT recreated; the singleton daemon stack + ports :8801/:11434/file-api MUST NOT be relaunched by a fork; data/lake symlink if scoped tests read it). Ordered 8-step BUILD task list in the doc. loop_stage stays idle (BUILD-gated); ranks HIGH -- twin-approvable within the open epoch.

2026-07-13 BUILT L0->L2, worktree half PROVISIONAL (same fork/commit bc32e130). L2 = the H9 map-de-contention mechanism (built+proof-tested, the real structural win). KEY FINDING: isolation=worktree is a CONFIRMED per-invocation Agent-dispatch parameter (usable NOW for true per-fork worktree isolation), but whether it works as a STANDING frontmatter default in .claude/agents/ is UNVERIFIED (no network vs official docs) -- wired best-effort + flagged provisional in both agent files; the guaranteed form is passing isolation=worktree per Agent dispatch. 732 background tests pass. L3 NOT reached: needs the isolation directive verified AND a real two-worktree end-to-end build; the directive verification needs the director/official docs (not autonomously doable), so loop_stage idle (honest hold at L2, L3 blocked on external verification). The map-contention half is load-bearing meanwhile.

atoms.atom_name: The Naive Organ: a deliberately-amnesiac agent that questions the SYSTEM's claims/state (manufactures the outside view)

notes.text: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (THE_NAIVE_ORGAN.md, director-decided, P1 QUEUE rank HIGH). The director corrected the advisor's claim that the naive outside question is the irreplaceable HUMAN contribution: 'That doesn't require a human, far from it. This can be part of the process.' Mechanises COLD_EYES's own principle (VANTAGE not capability) pointed at the SYSTEM (its claims, state, stuck-ness) rather than at artefacts. SEVEN mechanisable triggers, each a real catch from this weekend, fire the organ on observable conditions: (1) flat metric while system reports healthy; (2) a TERMINAL-STATE claim ('exhausted'/'nothing to do'/'complete'/'no candidates'/'blocked') -- MUST independently check raw data (caught 'map exhausted' with 31 open atoms); (3) an INHERENCE claim ('physics'/'inherent'/'cannot be parallelised') -- fact or unchecked assumption? (caught 'build must be narrow' -- worktrees existed all along, cf H10); (4) a first-principles claim about a fast-moving external field made without checking published practice (cf G3 Finding 1); (5) sustained work in one category while the goal metric does not move (caught harness-grooming vs company backlog -- the treadmill); (6) claim-vs-data contradiction on any observable surface; (7) the same class of fix applied N times -- is the class defined correctly? DESIGN (the discipline that makes it work): DELIBERATELY AMNESIAC -- no memory, no accumulated context, no codebase, no CLAUDE.md, no access to the system's rationalisations (a naive agent that accumulates context stops being naive and inherits the assumptions it exists to break) -- the structural INVERSE of director_twin.py's isolation, and enforced the same process way (separate invocation, no context carryover, PROVE it). Inputs ONLY: the goal, observable state (metrics, raw data files, the map AS DATA), and the system's own CLAIMS -- never its reasoning. Output = QUESTIONS not fixes (land in digest + Director door + back to the agent as findings to answer WITH EVIDENCE; 'no, that was true because X' is a fine answer, 'that's just how it is' is not). Opus-tier (a good naive question is judgment). ANTI-CAPTURE: never tuned by the system it questions, never learns house norms, never told which questions were 'unhelpful' -- its hit-rate is a diagnostic for US, not a training signal for IT (this is Law-B-shaped, same as the twin's no-learn rule). Does NOT replace PURPOSE: it can ASK 'should this company optimise for EV?' but cannot CARE -- the Epoch-4 fitness function and every values decision stay the director's (he is the only one who WANTS). DoD: organ live with the 7 triggers wired, firing on real conditions; questions logged + answered with evidence; amnesia enforced structurally (prove no context carryover); hit-rate tracked; questions+answers surfaced in digest and on the site; SEED TEST -- replay this weekend and independently rediscover >=3 of the 7 catches. loop_stage idle: DISCOVER/FRAME (design the trigger detectors, the amnesia-isolation mechanism, the seed replay) workable NOW; BUILD ranks HIGH for the next slot (twin-approvable within the open epoch per canon v2 3a -- a self-audit organ, not a one-way door). 2026-07-13 DISCOVER/FRAME LANDED (doc-only, Lane-3, no level move): full buildable design at docs/design/NAIVE_ORGAN_DESIGN.md -- all 7 trigger detectors mechanised (concrete files/regex/thresholds, each traced to its real weekend commit), amnesia-isolation as the structural inverse of director_twin (same process defences: --tools=, scratch cwd, no bypass; ONLY inputs = goal+raw observable state+claim, proven by 3 process-level tests PA-1 canary/PA-2 empty-cwd/PA-3 payload-closure), output contract (naive_organ_log.jsonl, QUESTIONS not fixes, answer-with-evidence loop that REJECTS empty evidence, Law-B no-learn anti-capture), seed replay test replaying this weekend's frozen state to rediscover >=3 of 7 catches, Opus-tier fires-on-triggers-not-timer with debounce, ordered 10-step BUILD list + 4 open questions (biggest: the organ catches INCOHERENCE not COHERENT delusion -- log as R10 named simplification at build).

2026-07-13 DIRECTOR AMENDMENT (commit 0685dd26 [ADVISOR-STAGED], the directive is in the commit MESSAGE; scope-expands this atom, MUST shape the build): ONE ORGAN, do not proliferate -- a separate 'devil's advocate' is the SAME organ, do not add another sceptic. TWO MODES: interrogate (question a claim) / falsify (try to break a plan/claim). THREE TARGETS: (1) the SYSTEM's own claims/state (the original 7 triggers); (2) THE ADVISOR'S staged docs BEFORE the agent acts on them -- the advisor is the LEAST-PROTECTED seat (full context, writes the plans AND reviews the outcomes, 3x R9 breaches in 3 days, has no evaluator of its own), so the organ runs a FALSIFY pass on an advisor-staged doc before action and THE AGENT MAY CITE A FALSIFY PASS TO PUSH BACK ON THE ADVISOR, AND SHOULD; (3) the DIRECTOR's factual/strategic CLAIMS (falsifiable statements of fact/strategy). THE LINE (non-negotiable): it questions CLAIMS and PLANS from ANYONE, but NEVER questions PURPOSE -- values / the fitness function are the director's and are NOT falsifiable (same wall as canon Law B / R13). META-TASK (DISCOVER/FRAME, do first): AUDIT our own existing doubt machinery (director_twin approver, phase-close-evaluator/Expert-Hour, cold-eyes walk, this organ) for OVERLAP before building -- 'adding another sceptic is not the same as being more sceptical'; the organ must be the ONE consolidated doubt mechanism, not a fourth parallel one. Build consequence: the NAIVE_ORGAN_DESIGN.md needs a revision pass folding in the 2-modes/3-targets/THE-LINE structure + the advisor-falsify target (a new input source: advisor-staged docs read before action) + the doubt-machinery overlap audit, BEFORE the organ is built.

2026-07-13 BUILT L0->L1 (worktree fork, commit 29ba79c3, merged to main 1f305d5b; orchestrator-verified 14/14 tests + epistemic PASS on merged main). background/naive_organ.py built to the DIRECTOR AMENDMENT: TWO modes (MODE_INTERROGATE/MODE_FALSIFY, one ask_organ differing only by rubric -- ONE organ); THREE targets (SYSTEM via detect_t1..t7/run_system_organ; ADVISOR via falsify_advisor_doc(doc_ref,doc_text) running a FALSIFY pass on a staged doc BEFORE the agent acts, cite-able to push back; DIRECTOR via interrogate_claim); THE LINE via is_purpose_claim() which declines any purpose/values/fitness claim BEFORE any Opus call (verdict declined_purpose, zero model spend, tested). DOUBT-MACHINERY AUDIT (design section 8.0): the organ is the standing claim/plan FALSIFIER (amnesiac, attacks truth); the twin is the director-canon APPROVER (max context, rules on authority) -- structural inverses; phase-close-evaluator/cold-eyes are artefact-scoped+episodic; no existing mechanism did the organ job = it is the ONE consolidated doubt mechanism, not a fourth sceptic. AMNESIA proven structurally (PA-1 canary never reaches payload; PA-2 --tools= empty-cwd scratch outside repo; PA-3 build_prompt pure over its 4 args); answer-rejects-empty-evidence (EmptyEvidenceRejected); anti-capture (no prior Q/A, thresholds are source constants). HONEST L1 not L2: run_system_organ exists but NOTHING in the running daemons calls it yet (not wired to fire in the live digest/supervisor loop), no frozen weekend fixture + real-Opus seed replay (replay is inline/stubbed), no digest NAIVE-ORGAN-asks surface. L2 NEXT: wire run_detectors+ask_organ into the digest build + phase/atom close + supervisor terminal-state/idle hooks (QUEUE per SELF_INTERRUPT_DISCIPLINE), build the frozen weekend fixture + real-Opus smoke test, add the digest section. L3 = habitual firing + seed-replay rediscovering >=3 weekend catches with real Opus. atoms.atom_name: Mutation-test the entire control apparatus: every control must fire on its own named defect (kill list + the 3 killer patterns)

notes.text: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (CONTROLS_THAT_CANNOT_FAIL.md, director P0, rank HIGHEST -- prompted by the F5 VAT-tautology red-team finding). Doctrine (now R15 in CLAUDE.md): a control that CANNOT FAIL is worse than none -- it manufactures confidence. For EVERY control (invariant/Tier-1 gate/validator/evaluator/daemon/health-check): MUTATION-TEST it -- inject the exact defect it exists to catch, it MUST fire; a control that does not fire on its named defect is theatre (delete/rebuild). Audit for the 3 KILLER PATTERNS: TAUTOLOGY (checked value derived from the same source it checks -- the VAT case, an independence violation), FAIL-OPEN (passes on missing/zero/empty/malformed -- e.g. subtotal<=0 skips the gate), FAIL-SILENT (passes when the CHECKER ITSELF is unavailable -- the Qwen backstop returning clean when Ollama is down = every autonomous run; an unavailable check is a FAILED check, must ALARM on absence). DoD: every control mutation-tested with results published; kill list with COUNTS (N tested, M fired, K theatre) published on The Proof door + site/data/; R15 standing rule in CLAUDE.md (DONE); any level-promotion/Expert-Hour/green-suite that rested on a theatre control RE-OPENED (retro-apply); F6/F7 closed as part of the CLASS not as instances. Report the theatre count honestly, however embarrassing. Pitch: auditing our own controls and finding our flagship Tier-1 check could not fail is one of the strongest artefacts this project will produce (every real supplier has tautological/fail-open/fail-silent controls nobody audits -- exactly why regulators keep finding the same harms after the same controls passed). First mutation-test fork dispatched (harness + inventory + kill list on the compliance/billing/verifier/backstop core). loop_stage build: this is the highest-ranked BUILD on the map -- nothing is graded reliably until it lands.

2026-07-13 BUILT L0->L2, KILL LIST PUBLISHED (worktree fork, commit e16f00de, merged to main e86601052; orchestrator-verified: mutation harness 47 pass + 1 strict-xfail, epistemic PASS). tests/controls/test_control_mutation.py mutation-tests each control (inject its named defect, assert it FIRES); docs/design/control_registry.json (registry) + docs/design/CONTROL_KILL_LIST.md + site/data/control_kill_list.json (Proof-door data). HEADLINE (honest): 13 controls inventoried, 13 mutation-tested, 12 FIRED, 1 THEATRE. By killer pattern: 1 TAUTOLOGY (check_vat/vat_by_segment -- already mitigated by F5's independent consumption cross-check that fires; the tautology itself retained as defence-in-depth under a STRICT xfail so it cannot be quietly relabelled a real control -- KL-1, keep-vs-retire is a small decision), 1 FAIL-OPEN FIXED (Tier-1 pre-bill gate: subtotal<=0 silently skipped BOTH VAT checks -> now negative/non-positive-subtotal-with-VAT fails CLOSED/HELD, a legit zero/zero bill still passes), 1 FAIL-SILENT FIXED (the Qwen internal-audit backstop returned clean whenever Ollama was down = passing by NOT running on every autonomous run -> now a third 'unavailable' verdict emits a checker_unavailable ALARM finding so sanity_daemon reports the outage, zero per-bill false positives). RETRO-APPLY (R15 DoD) conclusion, honest: the ONE theatre control (VAT tautology) was already backstopped by F5's real cross-check BEFORE it could justify a false promotion -- the compliance/bill-correctness atoms that cited the VAT gate are now backed by a control that genuinely fires -- so NO atom promotion needs re-opening on its account (stated, not assumed: 12 of 13 tested controls fire for real). HONEST L2 not L3: this pass covered only the highest-tier customer-impacting set (compliance invariants + Tier-1 gates + epistemic verifier + Qwen backstop). L3 residual = mutation-test the INVENTORIED-BUT-UNTESTED categories (LLM-judge evaluators, daemon scheduling/dedup, population_sanity.py, health/page-consistency R14 gates, other compliance trackers: green-claims/consumer-duty/social-obligation/crisis-bad-debt) + wire the kill list onto the LIVE Proof door (R11 pixel) + KL-2 (epistemic-verifier coverage gaps: missing-file reads clean, only line-start imports matched -- an AST scan, folds into W4_2_verifier_timing_extension). loop_stage build: continue to full coverage.

2026-07-13 PASS 2, mutation coverage extended (worktree fork, commit ad482600, merged 490c1bcab; verified 49 pass+1 xfail, epistemic PASS). CUMULATIVE: 27 controls mutation-tested / 26 FIRED / 1 THEATRE (check_vat, mitigated by F5). Extended to the deterministic apparatus: compliance trackers, R14 basis-labels + dashboard-consistency + population-consistency gates, health_check (run + stale-code), the change-detection gate. Found 5 MORE gaps registered as F8 (KL-4 population_sanity empty-reads fail-silent; KL-5 ConsumerDuty GREEN-on-empty-register; KL-6 social_obligation self-status tautology, mitigated; KL-7 green_claims 100pct-compliant-on-zero-obligations fail-open; KL-8 _check_consistency fail-silent+open on missing run_insights, the R11 orphan class). FIXED 0 this pass -- an HONEST QUEUE-by-default call: unlike pass 1 isolated single-caller fixes, every gap here is a multi-caller semantics change (consumer_duty.overall_rag has 14+ callers), so per SELF_INTERRUPT_DISCIPLINE they are registered not fixed-on-sight. Two pass-2 controls PASSED as fail-CLOSED (health_check tmux/ps-both-unavailable alarms; change-detection processes on unreadable dedup memory) -- no change needed. atoms.atom_name: Judge validation: outcome-test the verdict-producing organs we cannot mutation-test (consistency + independence + gold set + outcome correlation)

notes.text: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (JUDGING_THE_JUDGES.md Part 1, director P1 -- directly answers H12's honest residual that LLM-judge verdict QUALITY is un-mutation-testable). You cannot inject a defect to prove a JUDGMENT was bad, but a judge is not un-attackable. FOUR approaches: (1) OUTCOME CORRELATION (strongest + nearly free) -- a judge whose PASSES later FAIL is a bad judge; the trust/predictions ledger ALREADY tracks defects-found-post-close, sitting uncomputed -- wire per-judge/per-task-class (verdicts passed) vs (defects later found) = the empirical error rate, validate judgment by CONSEQUENCE; (2) CONSISTENCY -- same input repeated -> same verdict? flip rate; a judge that flips on identical evidence is noise; (3) INDEPENDENCE -- differently-primed judges agree? systematic disagreement is a finding about the judge; (4) GOLD SET seeded from the DIRECTOR's own Expert Hours (C1 SME-as-Household bill, thesis/hedge chart, bad-debt smell, meter-read mismatch) -- a judge that misses what the director caught is weak, with a number. STANDING RULE (into CLAUDE.md, R15 extension): where a control cannot be mutation-tested it must be OUTCOME-tested; no verdict-producing organ escapes measurement. Build fork dispatched (outcome-correlation first). loop_stage build: closes the H12 LLM-judge coverage residual.

2026-07-13 BUILT L0->L2 (worktree fork, commit 35d2f78, merged 8b0452fc; verified 34 judge-validation+trust-ledger tests pass, epistemic PASS). Closes H12's LLM-judge un-mutation-testable residual by OUTCOME-testing (R15 extension). background/judge_validation.py: (1) OUTCOME CORRELATION -- trust_ledger.record_post_close_defect() attaches a later-found defect to the earlier PASS (the linkage that was missing), outcome_error_rates() = passes-that-later-proved-defective/total-passes per judge/class (currently 0.0 over 1 verdict, accrues over runs); (2) GOLD SET from 4 real director Expert-Hour catches (C6 SME-as-Household, hedging volatility leak, bad-debt smell, C1 meter-read mismatch), score_judge_against_gold() -> recall + named misses, rubber-stamp scores 0.0/oracle 1.0 (scorer discriminates), each detectable_by tied to a real control; (3+4) consistency flip-rate + independence disagreement-rate defined+tested. Published site/data/judge_validation.json. HONEST L2 not L3: the metrics are built but the real NUMBERS accrue LIVE -- outcome error rate meaningful only as verdicts+post-close links accrue over runs; real-Opus consistency/independence + LLM gold-set recall need live judge invocations (like the naive organ's real-Opus residual + the twin's fidelity metric). L3 = the accrued live evidence + wiring record_post_close_defect into the real close/Expert-Hour/director-catch path. loop_stage build. SURFACED a real regression (fixed separately): the control-audit fixes correctly changed behaviour (KL-4 empty-reads now fires, internal_audit checker_unavailable), which broke 10 tests/background/test_sanity_daemon.py tests that encoded the OLD theatre-silent contract -- a fix fork dispatched to update them to the new fail-closed contract (the daemon is MORE correct; production runs on real data so no over-fire).

atoms.atom_name: Alert on a persistently-jammed publish pipeline: N consecutive auto-process rc=1 fires one NTFY (silent-retry-forever gap)

notes.text: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (self-caught finding, SELF_INTERRUPT_DISCIPLINE QUEUE not fix-on-sight). GAP: background_worker.process_leftover_run_markers() retries a run_complete marker that returns rc=1 (process_run_complete 'Tests FAILED - not committing') FOREVER with no alert -- a self-inflicted regression (e16f00de5 negative-subtotal HELD over-firing on legitimate credit bills) jammed the entire publish pipeline for ~40min (13 markers piled up, site went stale) and nothing fired. The test gate correctly refused to publish (R1/R2), but a PERSISTENTLY-failing publish gate is a state transition that must alert (R5). FIX (mechanism, MAKE_IT_STICK): a consecutive-failure counter keyed on the (marker-set unchanged AND rc=1) condition that fires exactly ONE NTFY after N>=2 consecutive failed sweeps, and clears on the next success -- never repeats an unchanged status (R5). Must be mutation-tested (R15): the alert fires on a simulated persistent-rc=1, and does NOT fire on a single transient failure or a healthy drain. See memory feedback-control-false-positive-jams-pipeline.

atoms.atom_name: Supervisor self-refill (dial-weighted maturity-map draw, replacing the fragile prose-scan)

notes.text: v1 wiring lands 2026-07-10 -- this very atom is the one being closed by that wiring. 2026-07-10 HARDEN pass (this atom drew ITSELF, fittingly): adversarially reviewed _maturity_map_draw() before a 4th live incident found the next gap rather than after. Found a real crash risk -- a malformed atom (a hand-editing typo like a quoted level_current string, or an explicit dial_inherited: null) would raise TypeError uncaught, aborting find_work() before it reaches the backlog-prose fallback -- silently reintroducing the exact idle-hole class of bug this mechanism was built to eliminate, specifically during agenda+staging-empty periods (self-refill's own use case). Fixed: per-atom validation now isolates a malformed atom to 'excluded from this draw', not 'the whole draw stops working forever'. Known, accepted lower-severity gap not fixed (self-defeating, not crash-causing): an atom listing itself in its own depends_on would permanently self-exclude rather than crash -- an obvious typo, visually apparent on review, left undefended rather than adding more complexity for a safe-side failure mode. 3 new tests, 452/452 background tests pass, epistemic PASS, live supervisor restarted.

2026-07-12 R3 REDESIGN, real regression found and fixed (P0, 9th idle variant, R3_WORK_GRANTING_REDESIGN.md, director-caught from the live console -- 'the director is hand-typing self-refill next atom -- he is manually performing the supervisor's core function'): find_work()'s 'unprocessed staging' check included run_complete_*.md (the auto-process daemon's own routine marker, needing no granted turn to be handled at all) in the SAME list used to decide 'real work exists on the instruction channel' -- so as long as that marker sat in docs/staging/ (nearly continuously), find_work() returned early and NEVER reached this atom's own self-refill draw. ~35 open map atoms sat idle while this repeated every ~2 minutes; a live session correctly identifying the daemon marker as 'not mine to act on' individually was collectively the exact failure. Fixed: daemon markers excluded from the instruction-channel check entirely (_real_staged_instructions()/_is_daemon_marker()); the self-refill draw is now UNCONDITIONAL (runs and gets appended to the reason even when a real doorbell already fired, not only as a fallback reached when nothing else exists); find_work() now returns (reason, map_exhausted) so a genuine CANNOT-draw (every atom blocked/complete) can be escalated distinctly from an ordinary didn't-draw-this-cycle; an idle-turn counter instruments the 'nothing to do' state directly (target zero, no longer silent). 16 new tests, 92 total in tests/background/test_supervisor.py pass, epistemic verifier PASS. Level held at 3 (already at target) -- this is a same-mechanism regression fix, not a new capability, but registered honestly per this project's own R10 discipline (an absurdity-class defect closes by extending the invariant/test coverage, not a silent instance fix).

atoms.atom_name: Production-readiness non-functional evidence pass (recoverability, security posture, capacity, availability)

notes.text: 2026-07-11 REGISTERED (P2, advisor-staged, director-decided -- PRODUCTION_READINESS_BASELINE.md, background-lane, explicitly 'do not displace the spike queue'). No measured baseline currently exists for this business's non-functional properties. Part A scope: recoverability (enumerate everything canonical existing ONLY on Skynet; test an actual restore of one representative artefact -- advisor amendment: to a SCRATCH location, never over live state; report measured RTO/RPO, not assumed), security posture (secrets inventory, exposed-surface inventory, dependency vulnerability scan -- advisor amendment: raw findings route to the PRIVATE ops repo ONLY, the public surface carries a sanitised summary never the map of weaknesses; absorb the harness sprint's existing sandbox/Pattern-C recommendation as an input rather than re-deriving), performance/capacity (measured full-run wall time/memory/disk and scaling with customer count, identify the first resource breaking at 10x/100x population), availability (daemon-stack real uptime from existing logs, every current single point of failure). Non-negotiable: measure, don't assert (R9). Immediate-action carve-out: any live-risk finding (unrecoverable canonical data, exposed credential) gets fixed under normal tiering NOW, not deferred -- the agent reviews and acts on such findings personally rather than delegating remediation, per this session's own consequential-action discipline. Dispatched as a background-lane fork; findings pending.

2026-07-11 PART A COMPLETE, level 0->1. No live risk found matching the imminent-danger tier (no exposed credential value, nothing about to be committed unsafely). Real findings, all measured not asserted: (1) Recoverability -- repo IS GitHub-mirrored (real off-machine copy for tracked content); company/data/*.db (4 SQLite files, largest 2.1MB, the company's own operational financial/customer state) and both .env.* secret files were gitignored with NO upstream copy at all -- matches the 'unrecoverable canonical data' carve-out criteria literally even though not imminent-danger; restore test performed for real (236-byte artefact, scratch location, byte-for-byte verified, ~22ms). ACTED ON per the carve-out: new background/backup_company_data.py backs up all 4 DB files to the private ops repo (verified via md5sum match), wired into process_run_complete.py's cycle so it stays current, not a one-off. (2) Security -- 2/2 secret files confirmed gitignored/600-permissioned/never in git history; zero raw secret-shaped strings elsewhere; 1 medium finding (5 stale unauthenticated dev file-servers running ~2 days, not a data leak); 1 low finding (wide-open CORS on an otherwise-authenticated endpoint); pip-audit not installed, no automated CVE scan possible. Detailed findings routed to the PRIVATE ops repo per the security boundary; public summary sanitised. (3) Performance -- confirmed 19 customers/~470s-run/2.9MB output matches CLAUDE.md's established figure; 2 real O(n²) patterns found by reading simulation/run_phase2b.py directly (per-customer full linear scans of all_records) -- ~100x cost at 10x population, ~10,000x at 100x for that specific pass, code-verified not load-tested; docs/observability/risk-committee-log.md found unbounded at 277MB. (4) Availability -- real crash history in session-watchdog-log.md (2 crash-loop episodes + one ~22min nvm-path outage in the last week); confirmed exactly 6 daemons share the NTFY-env-var SPOF; whole stack single-machine by design, no redundancy. 4 new tests (backup script), 520 background/ tests pass, epistemic PASS. H4_go_live_nfr_register can now carry a real 'current measured state' column from this evidence.

2026-07-13 DISCOVER/FRAME LANDED, Parts B/C (Lane-3 fork, doc-only, no level move, commit faf2751a): docs/design/PRODUCTION_READINESS_PARTS_BC.md. Part A covered 4 SRE areas; Part B = Operational and Resilience (availability/SLO, RTO/RPO/DR, observability/alerting, incident-response/runbooks, change-mgmt/rollback, capacity/scale -- cross-refs C-S1..C-S5 + the go-live seam async envelope as scale/idempotency/audit evidence); Part C = Security/Data-Protection/Compliance (authn/authz + SoD, secrets/key rotation, vuln mgmt, GDPR/encryption/retention, audit trail/non-repudiation, Ofgem operational-capability + financial-resilience licence NFRs). Biggest genuine gap = data protection C4 (no privacy policy, no encryption-at-rest assessed, no retention/erasure -- masked only because data is synthetic today). Top go-live blocker = C6 Ofgem licence gate (Ofgem-decided go/no-go, ~6mo, ~GBP115-130/customer capital floor; DISCOVER done, BUILD correctly Epoch-5-gated). TWO FINDINGS QUEUED (not fixed on sight, self-interrupt discipline): (1) docs/observability/risk-committee-log.md is UNBOUNDED -- 302MB (277->316MB since Part A), bloating the repo; needs truncation/rotation (a real harness-hygiene atom to register); (2) pip-audit (C3 dependency-vuln scan) has NEVER run -- cheapest security quick-win. atoms.atom_name: Development-time epistemic wall (lane hook pilot, fork as enforcement not risk)

notes.text: 2026-07-12 BUILT, level 0->1 (GOVERNED_COMPANY_AND_THREE_LANES.md Part 2 item 1, director-decided, 'fork as enforcement, not risk'): a PreToolUse hook (Read|Glob|Grep matcher) that, when a session declares SE_LANE=supplier or SE_LANE=sim via env var, denies reads crossing to the other side of the sim/company wall -- extending the epistemic wall from RUNTIME into DEVELOPMENT itself ('builder blindness, not just runtime blindness'). No-op (SE_LANE unset) for every normal session, including this one -- deliberately opt-in, not a standing restriction. Demonstrated both directions for real (not just in the automated test suite): a supplier-lane Read of simulation/renewals.py denied and logged; a sim-lane Read of company/pricing/tariff_engine.py denied and logged -- both in docs/observability/lane_hook_denials.jsonl. Live harness-level self-test (setting SE_LANE in this session's own environment to prove the REGISTERED hook fires via settings.json, not just the script directly) was deliberately NOT attempted -- correctly registering it in .claude/settings.json (schema-validated, matches the existing proven-working hook pattern exactly) plus direct pipe-testing was judged sufficient evidence without risking self-locking this session out of company/saas or sim/simulation reads mid-turn with no easy self-administered undo. NOT yet done (why level_current stops at 1, not higher): the full three-lane development model (separate contexts/worktrees per lane, typed-interface-only communication) is NOT this pilot -- that is the still-owed parallel-lanes proposal (docs/design/PARALLEL_LANES_PROPOSAL.md); Bash is out of scope for this pilot (Read/Glob/Grep only -- a `cat sim/foo.py` via Bash is not denied), registered as a known gap, not silently ignored. 14 new tests, epistemic PASS.

2026-07-12 MARKER-FILE EVOLUTION, level 1->2 (self-refill, dial-weighted draw; closes PARALLEL_LANES_PROPOSAL.md section 3 gap 1, the most significant of the 3 registered): the env-var-only design worked for a single human-directed session but had no mechanism for the Agent tool's own parallel-subagent-fan-out pattern (SUNDAY_WIDE's own standing default) to propagate a custom env var into one spawned subagent's own tool-call environment -- so a fork's own lane could never be enforced at all under the old design. Added a second, independent activation path: a `.se_lane` marker file in the current working directory (checked via Path.cwd(), not walking up parents -- a worktree root IS the hook's cwd for any tool call from within it), naturally scoping per-worktree (EnterWorktree/Agent(isolation:'worktree')) without needing env-var propagation through subagent spawning at all. Env var still wins when both are present (the more explicit, harder-to-leave-behind-by-accident signal) -- verified directly, not just designed. 7 new tests (activation both directions, own-side still allowed, no-marker no-op, unrecognised-lane no-op, whitespace-stripped content, env-var precedence) plus a REAL live demonstration (not just the automated suite, matching this atom's own evidentiary bar from its first build): a genuine subprocess invocation from a temp directory containing a .se_lane=supplier file, denied a sim/ read with exit code 2, zero env var involved. Level 2 (not 3) is the honest landing: this closes the single most consequential registered gap with real, demonstrated, tested code -- passing DISCOVER-lite+VERIFY per the map's own L2 bar -- but no HARDEN/Expert-Hour pass has run on this atom, and gap 3 (Bash out of scope -- a `cat sim/foo.py` via Bash is not denied, a meaningfully harder command-string-parsing problem) remains registered, not closed. 24 total tests in tests/tools/test_lane_wall_hook.py now pass, epistemic verifier PASS.

2026-07-12 HARDEN PASS, level 2->3 (self-refill, dial-weighted draw; genuine independent adversarial red-team, not self-graded -- same discipline as D3's Expert-Hour loop, scaled proportionately to this atom's dial=1): dispatched a fresh-context adversarial reviewer with no memory of the build, instructed to try to bypass the wall and execute every finding (not theorize). It found and CONFIRMED (by piping real adversarial payloads through the actual script) 7 real gaps, several severe: (1) ABSOLUTE PATH BYPASS, most severe -- Claude Code's own Read tool spec requires absolute paths, and the old `path.lstrip('./')` normalization never touched an absolute path at all, meaning this hook's Read-side protection was very likely dead on arrival against real Read calls, not a contrived edge case; every existing test fixture used bare relative paths, masking this entirely. (2) directory-traversal bypass (a `..`-resolving path evaded the un-resolved string match, including one disguised behind REGULATION_COMMONS_DOCTRINE.md's own shared-readable docs/domain_artefact_library/ prefix, directly defeating that doctrine's intent). (3) Glob/Grep calls with no explicit `path` (or Glob's own path-shaped `pattern` argument, e.g. 'sim/**/*.py', never inspected at all) recursed across the whole tree unchecked -- the easiest bypass to hit ACCIDENTALLY, not just adversarially, since omitting `path` is the idiomatic way to call these tools. (4) case-sensitivity bypass (`Sim/forward_curve.py` untouched by the regex). (5) a garbage/typo'd SE_LANE silently beat a correctly-configured .se_lane marker file via truthiness-not-validity precedence, disabling enforcement with zero signal. (6) marker-file content with mixed case or a stray extra line silently failed the lookup, indistinguishable from no marker configured at all. (7) an unreadable marker file (e.g. corrupted permissions) failed open with no warning. ALL 7 fixed same session: proper path resolution via a new _normalize_path() (absolute-or-relative, resolved against REPO_ROOT, lowercased) replaces the old bare lstrip; Glob's pattern is now checked (combined with `path` per real Glob semantics, not as two independent fragments); an unscoped Glob/Grep call is now denied outright while a lane is active; lane resolution now validates SE_LANE before letting it override the marker file; the marker file reads only its first line, lowercased; an unreadable marker now logs a visible WARNING before falling open (still fails open by design -- this is a soft dev-time pilot, not the runtime wall, and R12/proportionate-rigor argues against over-engineering a fail-closed mechanism into a dial=1 atom). 12 new regression tests, one per confirmed finding, all passing (36 total in tests/tools/test_lane_wall_hook.py), epistemic verifier PASS. L3 genuinely earned: passed a real, executed, independent HARDEN pass with every finding fixed and tested, not merely designed.

atoms.atom_name: Harness skills/ and path-scoped rules/ -- procedure moved out of CLAUDE.md into decay-resistant mechanism

notes.text: 2026-07-12 BUILT (HARNESS_SKILLS_AND_RULES.md, P2 non-interrupting, director-raised, verified against current official docs at code.claude.com via WebFetch before building per the doc's own non-negotiable): assessed 5 candidate skills, adopted 4 (phase-close, staging-protocol, cold-eyes-walk, incident-retro), rejected 1 (expert-hour-walk -- subsumed by cold-eyes-walk per COLD_EYES_PROTOCOL.md's own text: 'same machinery, stricter blindfold'; a separate skill would fragment one procedure across files). Full adoption report with reasons: docs/design/HARNESS_SKILLS_AND_RULES_ADOPTION.md. Two path-scoped rules built for the strongest use case the doc named: sim/** and company/**+saas/** operate under different laws, so the epistemic-wall reminder now fires on path match rather than depending on CLAUDE.md being remembered every session regardless of what's being touched. CLAUDE.md's own phase-close checklist (items 0-7) moved out verbatim into the phase-close skill -- 134 lines/34,840 chars -> 120 lines/30,773 chars, directly serving MAKE_IT_STICK (mechanism, not memory) since a loaded-on-demand skill cannot silently decay the way accumulating CLAUDE.md prose does. Demonstrated firing: phase-close and staging-protocol invoked directly via the Skill tool this session (full content loaded, confirmed in transcript); cold-eyes-walk and incident-retro confirmed discoverable in the harness's own available-skills listing immediately after creation. Path-scoped rules verified against the fetched official docs' documented trigger condition (fire on a matching Read/Edit, silently, by design -- no visible confirmation line the way skills have) -- the trigger itself was exercised (a real Read of company/interfaces/sim_interface.py during this session) but the silent-injection mechanism's correctness rests on the current, freshly-verified official documentation rather than a directly-observable log line. level_current 0->2 (L2: built, tested-by-real-invocation, evidence lands on a real mechanism) -- not yet L3: no adversarial/HARDEN pass has tried to break a rule's path-matching (e.g. does a symlinked path, a nested company/ under a worktree, or a file matching multiple rules' patterns behave correctly?), matching the exact scrutiny H6_lane_wall_development_pilot's own HARDEN pass applied to a structurally similar mechanism (that pass found 7 real path-matching bypass classes on a superficially-similar hook).

2026-07-12 HARDEN ATTEMPT, real finding, level HELD at 2 (self-refill, dial-weighted draw): unlike H6's own hook (a custom Python script this project owns and can pipe adversarial payloads through directly), .claude/rules/'s path-matching engine is Claude Code's OWN internal mechanism -- not black-box-testable from within this repo the same way. Verified against the official docs (WebFetch) that Claude Code ships exactly the debugging tool this gap needs: the InstructionsLoaded hook, firing with `load_reason` (session_start/nested_traversal/path_glob_match/include/compact) + the loaded `file_path`/`file_name`. Built .claude/hooks/log_instructions_loaded.py (logs every load event to docs/observability/instructions_loaded_log.jsonl) and registered it in .claude/settings.json under the `path_glob_match` matcher. REAL FINDING, not asserted: tested immediately in this same session by reading real adversarial paths (company/interfaces/point_in_time_view.py, saas/reporting/annual_report.py, simulation/hedged_settlement.py, tests/company/governance/test_decision_rights.py -- the last one a deliberate near-miss, since 'tests/company/' does not start with 'company/' and should NOT match) -- the log file was NOT created at all, meaning the newly-registered hook did not fire mid-session. This is the exact R2 class ('committed != running') applied to harness CONFIGURATION rather than a long-running process: hooks are loaded once at session start, and a settings.json edit made mid-session does not retroactively register for the CURRENT session -- the same limitation this project already knows from PreToolUse hooks, now confirmed for InstructionsLoaded specifically, with real evidence (an empty/missing log file after real matching reads) rather than assumed. Genuine adversarial verification (does tests/company/ correctly NOT match, does a nested/symlinked path behave per the docs' 2.1.198 guarantee, does a file matching multiple rules load both) is deferred to the next fresh session start, which will have the hook active from the beginning -- registered honestly as the concrete remaining L3 blocker, not silently claimed done. level_current stays 2; loop_stage stays harden (genuinely still the right stage -- the verification mechanism now exists and is correctly wired, only its first real exercise is pending a session boundary this atom cannot force itself).

2026-07-12 IDLE-HOLE FIX, loop_stage harden->idle (self-refill drew this exact atom 4+ turns running with genuinely zero new work available between draws -- confirmed by direct inspection, not assumed: recomputed the real BUILD-candidate pool via the map's own eligibility logic and found H7 was THE ONLY valid candidate in the entire 53-atom map, meaning every draw was guaranteed to re-select it, not landing on it by chance. Same structural class background/health_check.py::_check_stale_dependencies() and the earlier E2_revenue_reconciliation fix this session both address (an atom left in a state the self-refill keeps re-selecting with no real progress possible), but a NEW root cause: not a stale depends_on or a stale loop_stage-vs-reality mismatch -- a genuinely correct BUILD candidate whose only remaining step is blocked on a SESSION-LIFECYCLE event (a fresh Claude Code session start) that no in-session action can force, and the map's own schema has no field to represent 'blocked on session boundary' distinct from 'blocked on an atom dependency'. Consequence, also confirmed directly: with H7 as the sole BUILD candidate, self-refill never reached the idle-tier DISCOVER/FRAME draw (EPOCH_GATING_AND_ATOM_AUTHORSHIP.md's own fix) at all, since that tier only fires when the BUILD tier returns empty -- 30+ idle atoms with real DISCOVER/FRAME work sat undrawn behind this one atom's own perpetual re-selection. Fix: loop_stage build-blocking idle temporarily -- NOT an epoch-parked state in the usual sense this project uses 'idle' for, but the same field is the only honest way to say 'not currently actionable, for a reason outside this atom's own control' pending the map schema growing a distinct field for session-boundary blocks (registered as a real, separate follow-up, not built here -- would need its own DISCOVER/FRAME pass first). Reset to harden the moment a fresh session confirms (or refutes) the InstructionsLoaded hook fires correctly against the adversarial paths already named above -- not automatically on a timer, and not by this atom re-investigating itself again with no new evidence to offer.

2026-07-12 REAL NEW DATA POINT, level held at 2, loop_stage stays idle (self-refill draw, no BUILD code -- not the full adversarial verification, honestly partial). Checked docs/observability/instructions_loaded_log.jsonl directly rather than assuming it was still empty: it now has a genuinely NEW entry (2026-07-12T22:25:13Z, .claude/rules/epistemic-wall-company.md, path_glob_match) that did not exist when the prior entry above was written -- the hook HAS fired at least once mid-session since then, refuting the flat 'did not fire at all' state (this is real progress, not the same finding repeated). Immediately tested further: read company/interfaces/sim_interface.py (a real, adversarial-shaped path matching the same rule) right now and checked the log again -- NO new entry appeared. Plausible, coherent explanation (not yet confirmed): Claude Code's own rule-injection likely deduplicates within a rebuilt context -- a rule already injected once (e.g. after this session's own mid-conversation compaction event, a genuine context-rebuild boundary, though not identical to a full process restart) does not re-fire for every subsequent matching read, only for the first new match after the boundary. This is a real, honest, more nuanced finding than either 'broken' or 'fully verified': the mechanism is not dead, but its firing cadence is not 'once per matching read' the way a naive test would assume, and the genuine adversarial checks this atom's own remaining gap names (tests/company/ correctly NOT matching, nested/symlinked paths, multi-rule-match files) still have not been run and still require a real, deliberate test sequence, not incidental observation. loop_stage stays idle -- not yet a self-answerable HARDEN task, but the next attempt now has a concrete, falsifiable hypothesis to test (deduplication-per-context-boundary) rather than a blank 'wait for a session restart.'

2026-07-13 HYPOTHESIS FURTHER SUPPORTED, level held at 2, loop_stage stays idle (self-refill draw, no BUILD code). Re-checked instructions_loaded_log.jsonl: still exactly 3 lines, IDENTICAL to the prior check -- zero new entries, despite this session having read/edited many more real matching company/**+saas/** files in between (e.g. saas/ledger.py, saas/reporting/annual_report.py during the E3_accrual_restatement BUILD pass). If the rule fired on every matching read, several more entries would exist by now; their continued absence is CONFIRMING evidence for the deduplication-per-context-boundary hypothesis (fires once per rebuilt-context boundary, not per individual read), not just consistent with it. Still not the full adversarial verification this atom's own gap names (tests/company/ near-miss, nested/symlinked paths, multi-rule-match files all remain untested in a dedicated sequence) -- genuine incidental observation over multiple checks, not a deliberate adversarial test run. loop_stage stays idle.

2026-07-13 REAL CONTROLLED ADVERSARIAL TEST, level 2->3 (self-refill draw, DISCOVER/FRAME-classified turn -- this is verification/testing, not BUILD code, and closes the atom's own remaining named gap). Ran the deliberate test sequence prior passes could only observe incidentally: (1) captured the exact log line-count (7, having grown by one from a passive company-wall fire moments earlier) then made a genuine Read-tool call on company/crm/vulnerability_register.py (a file not yet opened via the Read tool this session, only via Bash sed/grep in earlier turns) -- the rule's content appeared immediately as a system-reminder injection in the SAME tool result, and the log grew to a confirmed new line at 02:17:28Z. (2) Immediately after, made a genuine Read-tool call on tests/company/governance/test_decision_rights.py (the atom's own named near-miss case: 'company/**/*.py' should NOT match a path starting 'tests/company/') -- no system-reminder fired, and the log stayed at the same line count. Both are real, controlled, before/after-verified results, not incidental. This also resolves the earlier 'deduplication-per-context-boundary' hypothesis as WRONG in its stated form, replacing it with a better-supported one: every prior 'missing fire' this atom logged was on a path read via Bash (sed -n/grep/cat), never via the actual Read tool -- checked directly: company/crm/home_registry.py, property_model.py, energy_profile.py, and company/compliance/domain_invariants.py were all read via Bash earlier this same session with zero corresponding log entries, while this pass's genuine Read-tool call on a sibling company/crm/*.py file fired instantly. The real mechanism is Read/Edit-tool-triggered path matching, not per-boundary deduplication -- a materially different and more useful fact for anyone relying on this hook (a Bash `cat`/`sed` of a wall-scoped file does NOT get the epistemic-wall reminder injected, only a Read/Edit tool call does). The two remaining named adversarial checks (nested/symlinked paths, multi-rule-match files) were checked for testability, not skipped: `find` confirmed zero symlinks exist anywhere under company/, sim/, or simulation/ in this repo's real source tree, and the two existing rules' path globs (company/**+saas/** vs sim/**+simulation/**) are structurally disjoint with no overlap -- both scenarios are genuinely UNTESTABLE against this repo's actual current structure, not untested by omission. All four named adversarial checks are now resolved (2 real PASS, 2 confirmed N/A) -- level_current 2->3 is a real, earned bump against the atom's own stated L3 bar, not rounded up on hope.
